case_name: Professional Conduct Committee appointed by the Physiotherapy Board of New Zealand v McLauchlan [2025] NZHPDT 1465 (21 March 2025)

summary.brief: Professional Conduct Committee appointed by the Physiotherapy Board of New Zealand v McLauchlan [2025] NZHPDT 1465 (21 March 2025). Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Between on or about 1 November 2021 and 3 November 2021, Ms McLauchlan did not make a sound professional judgement within her scope of practice and/or level of expertise
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms McLauchlan agreed to undertake a telehealth assessment for [Mr H] solely for the purpose of and/or in response to a request for a COVID-19 vaccine exemption
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The alleged conduct amounts to professional misconduct in that, either separately or cumulatively, it amounts to malpractice or negligence in relation to Ms McLauchlan’s scope of practice
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Negligence or malpractice may or may not be sufficient to constitute professional misconduct and the guide must be standards applicable by competent, ethical and responsible practitioners
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: failed to seek and/or arrange for any diagnostic blood tests and/or to refer [Mr H] for any such tests or further examination relating to the potential undiagnosed rheumatoid arthritis
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: failed to have regard to (i.e. refer to and/or consider) the Comirnaty datasheet which set out the potential risks and/or contraindications for the COVID-19 vaccine
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: By acting in the manner alleged above Ms McLauchlan was in breach of her professional and/or ethical obligations as a physiotherapist
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal finds that she relied heavily on the RMP and her misunderstanding of the significance of the 'missing information'.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: failed to ensure that she had appropriate and current training, knowledge, and competencies: (i) to practise in a field of practice which was new to her
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The conduct and her response raise concerns about her ability to operate as an independent practitioner, without her personal views clouding her professional judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient requested a telehealth consultation for a COVID-19 vaccine exemption due to potential undiagnosed rheumatoid arthritis.

focused_exam.musculoskeletal: Mild swelling in distal MCP joints and knuckles, reduced lumbar spine ROM

requestables.name: Diagnostic Blood Tests
requestables.description: Blood tests to investigate potential rheumatoid arthritis

reveal.clinical_significance: Would help confirm or rule out rheumatoid arthritis
reveal.decision_impact: Should have been ordered to support diagnosis

timeline_phases.name: Telehealth Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Initial telehealth consultation for vaccine exemption

decision_points.prompt: Should diagnostic tests be ordered to confirm rheumatoid arthritis?

options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to confirm diagnosis before issuing exemption
Issued exemption based on potential diagnosis without tests

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not order any tests
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed exemption was justified without tests

expected_action_court.description: Diagnostic tests should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Tests are necessary to confirm diagnosis

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to confirm diagnosis with tests
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Tests should have been ordered to confirm rheumatoid arthritis
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Professional misconduct due to lack of evidence-based practice

patient_outcome.description: Patient received a vaccine exemption without confirmed diagnosis
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Potential delay in appropriate diagnosis and treatment

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to order diagnostic tests

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Tests would have confirmed or ruled out rheumatoid arthritis

factual_timeline.event: Agreed to telehealth assessment for vaccine exemption

court_findings.finding: Professional misconduct due to lack of evidence-based practice
